kba_problem_summary: After an SLS1508 or CSLS download, SIS bypass/force alarms (BYPASS_ALM) and user-defined SIF alarms are republished with the timestamp of their first occurrence instead of the current time.
severity_justification: Corrupts timestamp integrity of safety-related bypass alarms in SIS modules, potentially misleading operators and compromising alarm audit trails for safety conditions.
occurrence_justification: Manifests on subsequent downloads only after a prior bypass/force event has occurred; requires a specific maintenance sequence.
detectability_justification: Incorrect timestamp is visible in the alarm list but is not flagged by any system error or alarm, requiring manual comparison with expected current time.

modifiers_applied.reason: KBA explicitly states no workaround is available
Directly affects SLS1508/CSLS bypass alarm publication
Hotfix included in NK-2400-0144 (v15.FP2) and NK-2400-0598 (v15.FP3)

architecture_level_description: Livello 1: Controllori e dispositivi SIS (SLS1508, CSLS, controller hardware)
workaround_description: Nessun workaround disponibile secondo la KBA
fix_reference: NK-2400-0144 (v15.FP2), NK-2400-0598 (v15.FP3); in sviluppo per v15.LTS, v15.FP1, v16.LTS
divergence_explanation: Emerson classifica come 'Product Issues' con azione al prossimo service interval; l'analisi eleva a Warning per il coinvolgimento SIS e assenza di workaround.
red_flags: Affects multiple controller families and versions
notes: Il difetto non compromette la logica di sicurezza SIS ma solo l'integrità storica degli allarmi di bypass. Per le versioni senza hotfix (v15.LTS, v15.FP1, v16.LTS) il rischio rimane più elevato fino all'aggiornamento.
